---
title: "Regional Controls — Forecast Comparison Dashboard"
format:
  dashboard:
    orientation: rows
    theme: cosmo
    scrolling: true
    output-file: index.html
    nav-buttons:
      - icon: github
        href: https://github.com
---

```{python}
#| output: false

from pathlib import Path
import yaml
import numpy as np
import pandas as pd
import plotly.graph_objects as go

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Load settings
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
with open("configs_pypyr/settings.yaml") as f:
    settings = yaml.safe_load(f)
base_year = settings["base_year"]
forecast_year = settings["forecast_year"]

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Discover available forecasts
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
archive_dir = Path("output/archive")
forecast_names = sorted(
    [d.name for d in archive_dir.iterdir() if d.is_dir()],
    reverse=True,
)

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Load PUMS base-year data (constant across forecasts)
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
with pd.HDFStore("data/pipeline.h5") as store:
    pums_hh = store["seed_households"]
    pums_persons = store["seed_persons"]

# --- Income quartile bins (dynamic, weighted) ---
weighted_income = pums_hh[["HINCP", "WGTP"]].dropna().copy()
weighted_income = weighted_income.loc[weighted_income["WGTP"] > 0].sort_values("HINCP")
cum_weight_share = weighted_income["WGTP"].cumsum() / weighted_income["WGTP"].sum()
weighted_cutoffs = np.interp([0.25, 0.5, 0.75], cum_weight_share, weighted_income["HINCP"])
income_bin_edges = [-np.inf, *weighted_cutoffs.tolist(), np.inf]
cutoff_k = np.rint(weighted_cutoffs / 1000).astype(int)
income_bin_labels = [
    f"up to ${cutoff_k[0]:,}k",
    f"${cutoff_k[0]:,}k–${cutoff_k[1]:,}k",
    f"${cutoff_k[1]:,}k–${cutoff_k[2]:,}k",
    f"${cutoff_k[2]:,}k+",
]

# --- Household size categories ---
size_categories = list(range(1, 8))
size_labels = [str(i) for i in range(1, 7)] + ["7+"]

# --- Workers per household categories ---
worker_categories = [0, 1, 2, 3, 4]
worker_labels = ["0", "1", "2", "3", "4+"]

# --- Age 5-year bins ---
age_bins = list(range(0, 90, 5)) + [float("inf")]
age_labels = ["0–4"] + [f"{i}–{i+4}" for i in range(5, 85, 5)] + ["85+"]

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Compute PUMS weighted distributions
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
pums_hh_c = pums_hh.copy()
pums_hh_c["income_quartile"] = pd.cut(
    pums_hh_c["HINCP"], bins=income_bin_edges, labels=income_bin_labels, include_lowest=True
)
pums_hh_c["hhsz"] = np.where(pums_hh_c["NP"] > 7, 7, pums_hh_c["NP"])
pums_hh_c["workers_capped"] = np.where(pums_hh_c["workers"] > 4, 4, pums_hh_c["workers"])

pums_income_counts = (
    pums_hh_c.groupby("income_quartile", observed=False)["WGTP"]
    .sum()
    .reindex(income_bin_labels, fill_value=0)
)
pums_income_props = (pums_income_counts / pums_income_counts.sum()).values

pums_size_counts = (
    pums_hh_c.groupby("hhsz")["WGTP"].sum().reindex(size_categories, fill_value=0)
)
pums_size_props = (pums_size_counts / pums_size_counts.sum()).values

pums_worker_counts = (
    pums_hh_c.groupby("workers_capped")["WGTP"].sum().reindex(worker_categories, fill_value=0)
)
pums_worker_props = (pums_worker_counts / pums_worker_counts.sum()).values

pums_persons_c = pums_persons.copy()
pums_persons_c["age_bin"] = pd.cut(
    pums_persons_c["AGEP"], bins=age_bins, labels=age_labels, right=False, include_lowest=True
)
pums_age_counts = (
    pums_persons_c.groupby("age_bin", observed=False)["PWGTP"]
    .sum()
    .reindex(age_labels, fill_value=0)
)
pums_age_props = (pums_age_counts / pums_age_counts.sum()).values

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Compute synthetic distributions for every forecast
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
synthetic_data = {}
for fc in forecast_names:
    fc_dir = archive_dir / fc
    syn_hh = pd.read_csv(fc_dir / "synthetic_households.csv")
    syn_per = pd.read_csv(fc_dir / "synthetic_persons.csv")

    syn_hh["income_quartile"] = pd.cut(
        syn_hh["HINCP"], bins=income_bin_edges, labels=income_bin_labels, include_lowest=True
    )
    syn_hh["hhsz"] = np.where(syn_hh["NP"] > 7, 7, syn_hh["NP"])
    syn_hh["workers_capped"] = np.where(syn_hh["workers"] > 4, 4, syn_hh["workers"])

    syn_income = syn_hh["income_quartile"].value_counts().reindex(income_bin_labels, fill_value=0)
    syn_income_props = (syn_income / syn_income.sum()).values

    syn_size = syn_hh.groupby("hhsz").size().reindex(size_categories, fill_value=0)
    syn_size_props = (syn_size / syn_size.sum()).values

    syn_workers = syn_hh.groupby("workers_capped").size().reindex(worker_categories, fill_value=0)
    syn_workers_props = (syn_workers / syn_workers.sum()).values

    syn_per["age_bin"] = pd.cut(
        syn_per["AGEP"], bins=age_bins, labels=age_labels, right=False, include_lowest=True
    )
    syn_age = syn_per.groupby("age_bin", observed=False).size().reindex(age_labels, fill_value=0)
    syn_age_props = (syn_age / syn_age.sum()).values

    synthetic_data[fc] = {
        "income": syn_income_props.tolist(),
        "size": syn_size_props.tolist(),
        "workers": syn_workers_props.tolist(),
        "age": syn_age_props.tolist(),
    }

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Convert to OJS-friendly structures
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
pums_data = {
    "income": pums_income_props.tolist(),
    "size": pums_size_props.tolist(),
    "workers": pums_worker_props.tolist(),
    "age": pums_age_props.tolist(),
}

ojs_define(
    forecast_names=forecast_names,
    income_labels=income_bin_labels,
    size_labels=size_labels,
    worker_labels=worker_labels,
    age_labels=age_labels,
    pums=pums_data,
    synthetic=synthetic_data,
    base_year=str(base_year),
    forecast_year=str(forecast_year),
)
```



# {.sidebar}

```{ojs}
viewof selected_forecast = Inputs.select(forecast_names, {
  label: "Forecast Scenario"
})
```

**PUMS** bars show the weighted base-year distribution (constant across scenarios).

**Synthetic** bars show the distribution from the selected forecast's PopulationSim output for the forecast year.

# Charts

## Row

### Column

```{ojs}
//| title: "Income Distribution"
{
  const syn = synthetic[selected_forecast];
  const pums_vals = pums.income;
  const labels = income_labels;
  const trace_pums = {
    x: labels,
    y: pums_vals,
    name: "PUMS " + base_year,
    type: "bar",
    marker: { color: "#054B6E" }
  };
  const trace_syn = {
    x: labels,
    y: syn.income,
    name: "Synthetic " + forecast_year,
    type: "bar",
    marker: { color: "#F05A28" }
  };
  const layout = {
    barmode: "group",
    height: 400,
    width: 650,
    yaxis: { title: "Proportion of Households", tickformat: ".1%" },
    legend: { orientation: "h", y: 1.12 },
    margin: { t: 30, b: 80 }
  };
  const div = DOM.element("div");
  Plotly.newPlot(div, [trace_pums, trace_syn], layout, {responsive: true});
  return div;
}
```

### Column

```{ojs}
//| title: "Household Size"
{
  const syn = synthetic[selected_forecast];
  const pums_vals = pums.size;
  const labels = size_labels;
  const trace_pums = {
    x: labels,
    y: pums_vals,
    name: "PUMS " + base_year,
    type: "bar",
    marker: { color: "#054B6E" }
  };
  const trace_syn = {
    x: labels,
    y: syn.size,
    name: "Synthetic " + forecast_year,
    type: "bar",
    marker: { color: "#F05A28" }
  };
  const layout = {
    barmode: "group",
    height: 400,
    width: 650,
    yaxis: { title: "Proportion of Households", tickformat: ".1%" },
    legend: { orientation: "h", y: 1.12 },
    margin: { t: 30, b: 50 }
  };
  const div = DOM.element("div");
  Plotly.newPlot(div, [trace_pums, trace_syn], layout, {responsive: true});
  return div;
}
```

## Row

### Column

```{ojs}
//| title: "Workers per Household"
{
  const syn = synthetic[selected_forecast];
  const pums_vals = pums.workers;
  const labels = worker_labels;
  const trace_pums = {
    x: labels,
    y: pums_vals,
    name: "PUMS " + base_year,
    type: "bar",
    marker: { color: "#054B6E" }
  };
  const trace_syn = {
    x: labels,
    y: syn.workers,
    name: "Synthetic " + forecast_year,
    type: "bar",
    marker: { color: "#F05A28" }
  };
  const layout = {
    barmode: "group",
    height: 400,
    width: 650,
    yaxis: { title: "Proportion of Households", tickformat: ".1%" },
    legend: { orientation: "h", y: 1.12 },
    margin: { t: 30, b: 50 }
  };
  const div = DOM.element("div");
  Plotly.newPlot(div, [trace_pums, trace_syn], layout, {responsive: true});
  return div;
}
```

### Column

```{ojs}
//| title: "Age Distribution (5-Year Bins)"
{
  const syn = synthetic[selected_forecast];
  const pums_vals = pums.age;
  const labels = age_labels;
  const trace_pums = {
    x: labels,
    y: pums_vals,
    name: "PUMS " + base_year,
    type: "bar",
    marker: { color: "#054B6E" }
  };
  const trace_syn = {
    x: labels,
    y: syn.age,
    name: "Synthetic " + forecast_year,
    type: "bar",
    marker: { color: "#F05A28" }
  };
  const layout = {
    barmode: "group",
    height: 400,
    width: 650,
    yaxis: { title: "Proportion of Persons", tickformat: ".1%" },
    xaxis: { tickangle: -45 },
    legend: { orientation: "h", y: 1.12 },
    margin: { t: 30, b: 80 }
  };
  const div = DOM.element("div");
  Plotly.newPlot(div, [trace_pums, trace_syn], layout, {responsive: true});
  return div;
}
```

```{ojs}
//| output: false
Plotly = require("https://cdn.plot.ly/plotly-2.35.2.min.js")
```
